steps.title: Read the graph trends
steps.content: The graph shows employment in agriculture, hunting, and forestry from 1995 to 2007. The Czech Republic line drops sharply (roughly 225 thousand to roughly 75 thousand employees), while Austria’s line declines only slightly (roughly 125 thousand to roughly 100 thousand), staying relatively flat compared to the Czech Republic.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — the majority of employees in Austria were working in these sectors.: Makes a claim about the proportion of Austria’s total workforce that cannot be determined from this graph, which shows raw numbers only. ✗
  • Choice B — the number of employees working in these sectors decreased in the Czech Republic while remaining relatively steady in Austria.: Accurately captures the sharp Czech Republic decline and Austria’s near-stability. ✓
  • Choice C — employment in these sectors remained stable in both Austria and the Czech Republic.: Incorrect for the Czech Republic, which shows a dramatic decrease, not stability. ✗
  • Choice D — the number of employees working in these sectors increased in the Czech Republic.: Reverses the Czech Republic’s direction — the graph shows a decrease, not an increase. ✗
That is the correct answer.
